subject: gain correction and 3d classification
body:
Dear Alister,

Thank you for developing such a great tool!  have a couple of questions 
that I’d really appreciate your help with:

1. After completing all the refinements in M, is there any way to bring all 
the particles back into Relion for 3D classification? what would be the 
best approach for that?

2. I was double-checking the gain correction to ensure it's correct for our 
setup. I estimated the gain by summing up all the TIFF files and checking 
the output (I've attached the .py file, which is not inverted). When I 
compared the achieved result (mygain.mrc) with the gain.mrc from the 
apoferritin data, they were identical. My question is: Why do we need to 
flip the data using --gain_flip_y?
 
(cause when I estimate the gain in the same way for our Falcon 4i detector 
(data acquired with Tomo5), the result is flipped in both the X and Y axes 
compared to the microscope output)

I appreciate your guidance and help.

Best regards,
Amin
